entry.title: FIAP SHIFT: Multicloud Architecture
entry.content:
A Computa├з├гo em Nuvem est├б transformando a forma como as organiza├з├╡es investem no mundo digital. Nesse cen├бrio, adotar Cloud Computing no ambiente corporativo ├й seguir o fluxo tecnol├│gico natural. E se manter sempre ├а frente quanto ├а e-science e op├з├╡es de servi├зos.

Al├йm de ser uma solu├з├гo inovadora, a Cloud tamb├йm ├й um modelo de neg├│cio relativamente novo, aplic├бvel a qualquer tipo de empresa. Nosso foco ├й formar o profissional para dominar modelos de servi├зos de Computa├з├гo em Nuvem (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS), modelos de implanta├з├гo em Nuvem (privado, p├║blico, h├нbrido e multicloud) e economia em Nuvem. Tudo isso por meio de atividades hands-on na Amazon Web Services (AWS).

A jornada ├й desafiadora. E a transforma├з├гo, o ├║nico caminho.

Para quem?

Profissionais de TI (analistas, especialistas e gestores) que atuam nas ├бreas de Cloud, Virtualiza├з├гo e/ou DevOps.

Pr├й-requisito

Recomendamos ter conhecimentos t├йcnicos gerais de TI e de neg├│cios.

Benef├нcio Amazon

Cada aluno vai receber US$ 50 em cr├йditos para usar em laborat├│rios no ambiente Amazon, durante e ap├│s o curso.

Shift remoto

Aulas ao vivo com conte├║do e metodologia especialmente pensados para o aprendizado remoto. Os v├нdeos dos encontros tamb├йm ficam dispon├нveis por 7 dias ap├│s a ├║ltima aula. O conte├║do complementar pode ser acessado durante 1 ano. E voc├к ainda tem acesso ao ecossistema FIAP e aos nossos coworkings.

Conte├║do

  • M├│dulo 1: Vis├гo geral dos conceitos de nuvem
    • Virtualiza├з├гo
    • Modelos de servi├зo
    • Seguran├зa em nuvem / Modelo de responsabilidade compartilhada
    • Modelos de implementa├з├гo
    • Multicloud
    • Economia e faturamento da nuvem
    • Arquitetura de nuvem / Desenvolvimento de aplica├з├╡es para a nuvem
  • M├│dulo 2: Infraestrutura como servi├зo (IaaS)
    • Instancia├з├гo de m├бquinas virtuais
    • Autoscaling
    • Infraestrutura de armazenamento: block/object/file storage
    • Containers
    • Orquestra├з├гo de containers
    • Arquitetura de microservi├зos
  • M├│dulo 3: Plataforma como servi├зo (PaaS)
    • Automa├з├гo e entrega cont├нnua (CI/CD)
    • Pipeline DevOps
    • DBaaS SQL/NoSQL
    • Function as a Service
    • Arquiteturas serverless

entry.title: FIAP SHIFT: AWS Academy Cloud Foundations

link.title: FIAP SHIFT: AWS Academy Cloud Foundations

entry.content:
O AWS Academy Cloud Foundations (Fundamentos de nuvem da AWS Academy) ├й destinado a profissionais interessados em aperfei├зoar seus conhecimentos sobre computa├з├гo em nuvem e AWS, e ├аqueles que gostariam de obter a certifica├з├гo da Amazon тАУ AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner.

O curso oferece uma vis├гo geral detalhada dos conceitos de nuvem, dos servi├зos essenciais, da seguran├зa, da arquitetura, da defini├з├гo de pre├зo e do suporte da AWS. A certifica├з├гo AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner ├й uma etapa recomendada para a obten├з├гo da certifica├з├гo Specialty ou em n├нvel Associate.

Para quem?

Profissionais DevOps e Arquitetos de Plataformas/Solu├з├╡es que querem ter certifica├з├гo e experi├кncia hands-on na AWS.

Pr├й-requisito

Recomendamos ter conhecimentos t├йcnicos gerais de TI e de neg├│cios.

Como funciona?

Voc├к vai ter acesso ├а plataforma AWS Academy, com videoaulas em ingl├кs (tradu├з├гo e legendas em portugu├кs), laborat├│rios para cada m├│dulo, leituras complementares e testes ap├│s cada etapa para acompanhar a evolu├з├гo com vista ao exame de certifica├з├гo. Mais 30 dias de acesso ├аs aulas, com dois momentos de intera├з├гo ao vivo com Jos├й Castillo, Accredited Educator da Amazon e professor da FIAP.

Digital + Live

Ser├гo 20 horas de curso, com dura├з├гo de quatro semanas, seis laborat├│rios pr├бticos e mais duas lives com o professor, visando ├а prepara├з├гo para a prova, nos dias 15/10 e 19/11, das 19h ├аs 20h. No fim, voc├к receber├б um certificado oficial de conclus├гo de curso da AWS Academy e poder├б aproveitar 50% OFF para fazer a prova de Certifica├з├гo AWS Cloud Foundations.

Conte├║do

  • M├│dulo 1: Vis├гo geral dos conceitos de nuvem
  • M├│dulo 2: Economia e faturamento da nuvem
  • M├│dulo 3: Vis├гo geral da infraestrutura global da AWS
  • M├│dulo 4: Seguran├зa na nuvem AWS
  • M├│dulo 5: Redes e entrega de conte├║do
  • M├│dulo 6: Computa├з├гo
  • M├│dulo 7: Armazenamento
  • M├│dulo 8: Bancos de dados
  • M├│dulo 9: Arquitetura de nuvem
  • M├│dulo 10: Auto scaling e monitoramento

entry.title: Huawei ICT Academy
entry.content:
A Huawei ICT Academy ├й um programa de parceria sem fins lucrativos que autoriza universidades e faculdades a oferecer cursos de Certifica├з├гo Huawei para estudantes. Este programa atua como uma ponte entre empresas e academia para construir um ecossistema de talentos para as ind├║strias de TIC.

Desde sua afilia├з├гo no programa em 2016, a Escola Polit├йcnica da USP oferece treinamento de certifica├з├гo t├йcnica para estudantes de institui├з├╡es de ensino superior, formando com sucesso mais de 200 estudantes nos cursos certificados pela HCIA.

  • Huawei ICT Competition Brazil 2018-2019
  • 13a edi├з├гo treinamento R&S
  • 12a edi├з├гo
  • 11a edi├з├гo
  • 10a edi├з├гo
  • 9a edi├з├гo
  • 8a edi├з├гo
  • 7a edi├з├гo
  • 5a/6a edi├з├╡es
  • 4a edi├з├гo
  • 3a edi├з├гo
  • 2a edi├з├гo
  • 1a edi├з├гo

Benef├нcios do programa

  • Participa├з├гo na Huawei ICT Competition
  • Vochers para fazer os exames de certifica├з├гo sem custo
  • Acesso ao portal da Huawei ICT Academy, permitindo o aprendizado a qualquer hora, em qualquer lugar

Certifica├з├гo Huawei

A Huawei criou um sistema de certifica├з├гo de tr├кs n├нveis:

  • Huawei Certified ICT Associate (HCIA)
  • Huawei Certified ICT Professional (HCIP)
  • Huawei Certified ICT Expert (HCIE)

Cada curso inclui:

  • Materiais de e-Learning
  • Exerc├нcios pr├бticos de laborat├│rio baseados em cen├бrios reais
  • V├нdeos que abrangem conhecimentos mais detalhados em ├бreas chave

Plataforma on-line

A plataforma on-line da Huawei ICT Academy fornece um suporte digital integrado com compartilhamento de recursos, suporte ao ensino, intera├з├гo com a comunidade e aprendizado m├│vel.

Os alunos podem adquirir materiais de aprendizagem, concluir tarefas, consultar resultados e interagir com professores e outros aprendizados a qualquer hora e em qualquer lugar.

Para se registrar na plataforma de aprendizado on-line gratuitamente e obter os materiais mais recentes: https://www.huaweiacad.com

entry.title: AWS Academy
entry.content:
Benef├нcios do programa

  • Acesso ao curr├нculo criado pela AWS: O curr├нculo pronto para ensino permite que as institui├з├╡es membro incorporem a instru├з├гo em seu cat├бlogo de cursos. As atualiza├з├╡es regulares da AWS refletem o ritmo da inova├з├гo e as melhores pr├бticas da computa├з├гo em nuvem.
  • Crie habilidades alinhadas ├а AWS Certification: os alunos adquirem habilidades de computa├з├гo na Nuvem AWS por meio de experi├кncia pr├бtica e podem se preparar para a AWS Certification com simulados gratuitos e descontos de 50% para os exames completos.
  • Aumente a for├зa de trabalho da Nuvem AWS: Com o curr├нculo da AWS Academy, os alunos obt├кm experi├кncia pr├бtica trabalhando em ambientes ao vivo, o que os ajuda a ganhar a experi├кncia do mundo real necess├бria para conseguir emprego no setor.

Cursos

AWS Academy Cloud Foundations

O curso ├й introdut├│rio e oferece uma vis├гo geral detalhada dos seguintes t├│picos:

  • conceitos da nuvem
  • principais servi├зos da AWS
  • seguran├зa
  • arquitetura
  • defini├з├гo de pre├зo
  • suporte.

O curso possui 20 horas de conte├║do e prepara os alunos para conseguir a certifica├з├гo AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner.

AWS Academy Cloud Developing (coming soon)

Esse curso de n├нvel intermedi├бrio ajudar├б os estudantes a ganharem experi├кncia t├йcnica em desenvolvimento com tecnologia de nuvens. Ele tamb├йm os ajudar├б a se preparar para o exame de certifica├з├гo AWS Certified Developer - Associate. Ao completar, os alunos estar├гo aptos a desenvolver com AWS SDK e identificar as melhores pr├бticas para construir e implantar aplicativos na nuvem AWS. Esse curso tem aproximadamente 40 horas de conte├║do fornecido por meio de palestras e laborat├│rios pr├бticos.

entry.title: Huawei Certificate Training Program
entry.content:
A Huawei est├б oferecendo treinamentos digitais gratuitos, trazendo para o Brasil o Huawei Certificate Training Program, um programa de treinamentos para realizar os cursos preparat├│rios das certifica├з├╡es n├нvel Associate, testes de qualifica├з├гo e solicitar um voucher para realizar a prova sem custo (que normalmente tem um valor de $200).

S├гo 500 vouchers para realiza├з├гo da prova, que pode ser realizada remotamente pelos centros da Person VUE.

entry.title: Importing alarms from OpenStack Aodh into Sensu Core

link.title: Importing alarms from OpenStack Aodh into Sensu Core

entry.content:
aodh2sensu

Imports OpenStack Aodh alarms into Sensu Core Server.

Install

Via requirements file

$ pip3 install -r ./requirements.txt

Via pipenv pipfile

$ pipenv install

Use

Usage: aodh2sensu.py [options]

Imports OpenStack Aodh alarms into Sensu Core Server.

Options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --sensu-url URL       if not specified, defaults to localhost:4567

  • Run the aodh2sensu proxy. sensu_url must point to the sensu server. The proxy must be run in a server reachable from OpenStack controllers and with access to the Sensu Server (for example, the Sensu Server itself).

     $ ./aodh2sensu.py

    or alternatively via pipenv:

     $ pipenv shell
 $ ./aodh2sensu.py
  • Create an Aodh alarm from OpenStack side. This example alarm will trigger an HTTP POST message to the aodh2sensu proxy whenever the cpu utilization of $INSTANCE_ID goes above 20%: 
     $ openstack alarm create \
 --name cpu_hi \
 --type gnocchi_resources_threshold \
 --description 'CPU High Average' \
 --metric cpu_util4 \
 --threshold 20.0 \
 --comparison-operator gt \
 --aggregation-method mean \
 --granularity 300 \
 --evaluation-periods 1 \
 --resource-type instance \
 --resource-id $INSTANCE_ID \
 --alarm-action 'http://x.y.z.w:50000' \
 --ok-action 'http://x.y.z.w:50000' \
 --insufficient-data-action 'http://x.y.z.w:50000'

    where x.y.z.w is the IP address of the server running aodh2sensu proxy.

  • Confirm the alarm transitions from insufficient_data state to ok state: 
     $ openstack alarm list
 +--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+----------------+-------------------+----------+---------+
 | alarm_id                             | type                                       | name           | state             | severity | enabled |
 +--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+----------------+-------------------+----------+---------+
 | c466d832-cfce-4488-9726-c631800a36b1 | gnocchi_resources_threshold                | cpu_hi4        | ok                | low      | True    |
 +--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+----------------+-------------------+----------+---------+
  • Generate load in the instance above the 20% threeshold, and wait for the alarm to transitition to alarm state: 
     $ openstack alarm list
 +--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+----------------+-------------------+----------+---------+
 | alarm_id                             | type                                       | name           | state             | severity | enabled |
 +--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+----------------+-------------------+----------+---------+
 | c466d832-cfce-4488-9726-c631800a36b1 | gnocchi_resources_threshold                | cpu_hi4        | alarm             | low      | True    |
 +--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+----------------+-------------------+----------+---------+

  • Check the alarm from the Uchiwa dashboard:

  • Stop the load generation in the instance, wait for the alarm to transition back to ok state: 
     $ openstack alarm list
 +--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+----------------+-------------------+----------+---------+
 | alarm_id                             | type                                       | name           | state             | severity | enabled |
 +--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+----------------+-------------------+----------+---------+
 | c466d832-cfce-4488-9726-c631800a36b1 | gnocchi_resources_threshold                | cpu_hi4        | ok                | low      | True    |
 +--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------+----------------+-------------------+----------+---------+

  • Check the new state of the sensu alert:

  • Confirm it has disappeared from the list of active alerts:

Docker

To build the image:

$ buildah build-using-dockerfile -t aodh2sensu .

To run the image:

$ podman run --name aodh2sensu --add-host="localhost:10.88.0.1" -d -p 50000:50000 aodh2sensu

Pointing to a specific Sensu Server URL:

$ podman run --name aodh2sensu --add-host="localhost:10.88.0.1" -d -p 50000:50000 -e SENSU_URL=x.y.z.w:p aodh2sensu

Check logs:

$ podman logs aodh2sensu

Systemd

To run aodh2sensu as a systemd service.

Normal

# cat /usr/lib/systemd/system/aodh2sensu.service
[Unit]
Description=aodh2sensu service

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/opt/aodh2sensu/aodh2sensu.py

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Docker

# cat ~/.config/systemd/user/aodh2sensu.service 
[Unit]
Description=aodh2sensu service via podman

[Service]
Restart=on-failure
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/rm -f /%t/%n-pid /%t/%n-cid
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --conmon-pidfile  /%t/%n-pid  --cidfile /%t/%n-cid --add-host="localhost:10.88.0.1" -d --name aodh2sensu -p 50000:50000 aodh2sensu
ExecStop=/usr/bin/sh -c "/usr/bin/podman rm -f `cat /%t/%n-cid`"
KillMode=none
Type=forking
PIDFile=/%t/%n-pid

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

entry.title: OpenStack Ussuri Contributor List
entry.content:
Thanks to the more than 1,000 contributors from more than 50 countries and 188 organizations that contributed to the OpenStack Ussuri release.

https://www.openstack.org/software/ussuri/

entry.title: OpenShift 4.3 on RHOSP 13: Installation and Integrations

link.title: OpenShift 4.3 on RHOSP 13: Installation and Integrations

entry.content:
I would like to share my experience installing and integrating OpenShift with various OpenStack services, namely Cinder, Swift and Keystone (Neutron and Octavia TBD).

Prerequisites

  • This was not a disconnected environment. Note that virtual machines have to be born with proper DNS configuration for OpenShift to download proper CoreOS images (NeutronDhcpAgentDnsmasqDnsServer┬аparameter from Director).
  • On Ceph RGW, the account in url┬аoption must be enabled (rgw_swift_account_in_url: true┬аparameter from Director).

Installation

Following official documentation:┬аhttps://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/openshift_container_platform/4.3/html-single/installing_on_openstack/index

Prepared a bastion server within the OpenShift project for convenience, downloaded into it the OpenStack credentials file and added the password into it.

[cloud-user@bastion ~]$ ./openshift-install create install-config --dir=installationDoc
? SSH Public Key /home/cloud-user/.ssh/id_rsa.pub
? Platform openstack
? Cloud openstack
? ExternalNetwork public
? APIFloatingIPAddress 192.168.226.42
? FlavorName openshift
? Base Domain openshift.com.br
? Cluster Name lab01
? Pull Secret [? for help] тАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН

The resulting configuration:

[cloud-user@bastion ~]$ cat installationDoc/install-config.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
baseDomain: openshift.com.br
compute:
- hyperthreading: Enabled
┬а name: worker
┬а platform: {}
┬а replicas: 1
controlPlane:
┬а hyperthreading: Enabled
┬а name: master
┬а platform: {}
┬а replicas: 1
metadata:
┬а creationTimestamp: null
┬а name: lab01
networking:
┬а clusterNetwork:
┬а - cidr: 10.128.0.0/14
┬а┬а┬а hostPrefix: 23
┬а machineCIDR: 10.0.0.0/16
┬а networkType: OpenShiftSDN
┬а serviceNetwork:
┬а - 172.30.0.0/16
platform:
┬а openstack:
┬а┬а┬а cloud: openstack
┬а┬а┬а computeFlavor: openshift
┬а┬а┬а externalDNS: null
┬а┬а┬а externalNetwork: public
┬а┬а┬а lbFloatingIP: 192.168.226.42
┬а┬а┬а octaviaSupport: "0"
┬а┬а┬а region: ""
┬а┬а┬а trunkSupport: "1"
publish: External
pullSecret: '{"auths":{"cloud.openshift.com":{"authтАЭ:тАЭabc==","email":"jlema@redhat.com"},"quay.io":{"authтАЭ:тАЭabc==","email":"jlema@redhat.com"},"registry.connect.redhat.com":{"authтАЭ:тАЭabc==тАЭ,тАЭemail":"jlema@redhat.com"},"registry.redhat.io":{"authтАЭ:тАЭabc==,тАЭ,тАЭemail":"jlema@redhat.com"}}}'
sshKey: |
┬а ssh-rsa abc== cloud-user@bastionтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН

As opposed to what the documentation says regarding the number of replicas (a┬аpositive integer greater than or equal to┬а3 for control nodes and greater to 2 for worker nodes), the installation actually works with┬а1 control node and 1 worker.

Proceed with the actual installation:

[cloud-user@bastion ~]$ ./openshift-install create cluster --dir=installationDoc --log-level=debug тАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН
First try we got this error:
...
INFO Creating infrastructure resources...┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а 
...

INFO Waiting up to 30m0s for the Kubernetes API at https://api.lab01.openshift.oss.timbrasil.com.br:6443... 
ERROR Attempted to gather ClusterOperator status after installation failure: listing 
INFO Pulling debug logs from the bootstrap machine 
ERROR Attempted to gather debug logs after installation failure: failed to create SSH client, ensure the proper ssh key is in your keyring or specify with --key: dial tcp 192.168.226.40:22: connect: connection refused 
FATAL Bootstrap failed to complete: waiting for Kubernetes API: context deadline exceeded

It was a MTU issue, as our environment has jumbo frames as its default. Actually, there is no way to set MTU in IPI installations, and it was decided to wait┬аto a future feature where you will be able to create your networking in OpenStack and then instruct the installer to use the existing networking resources while still installing with the IPI workflow (something like bring your own network).

The (not very elegant) solution was to wait for the network creation, and manually configure the MTU, before the virtual machines are created:

[cloud-user@bastion ~]$ watch -n 10 openstack network list
[cloud-user@bastion ~]$ openstack network set --mtu 1400 lab01-n67mh-openshiftтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН

Another issue we found at this point it is that for some reason the openshift-installer┬аdoes not use soft anti-affinity policies (different from AWS and from OpenStack UPI installation), and may create all the masters/workers in the same server. The only option to accomplish this would be to┬аdefine different regions for the nodes, which is not supported from RHOSP side.

Finally, installation completed:

Cinder Integration

Worked out of the box:

[cloud-user@bastion ~]$ oc get storageClass
NAME┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а TYPE
standard (default)┬а┬а kubernetes.io/cinderтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН

[cloud-user@bastion ~]$ oc describe storageClass standard
Name:┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а standard
IsDefaultClass: Yes
Annotations:┬а┬а┬а storageclass.kubernetes.io/is-default-class=true
Provisioner:┬а┬а┬а kubernetes.io/cinder
Parameters:┬а┬а┬а┬а <none>
Events:┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а <none>тАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН

[cloud-user@bastion ~]$ oc get -o yaml storageClass standard
allowVolumeExpansion: true
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
┬а annotations:
┬а┬а┬а storageclass.kubernetes.io/is-default-class: "true"
┬а creationTimestamp: 2020-03-24T18:17:11Z
┬а name: standard
┬а ownerReferences:
┬а - apiVersion: v1
┬а┬а┬а kind: clusteroperator
┬а┬а┬а name: storage
┬а┬а┬а uid: 82643882-1e38-4fdd-98c2-fb19636ad003
┬а resourceVersion: "10307"
┬а selfLink: /apis/storage.k8s.io/v1/storageclasses/standard
┬а uid: 7bb9919c-2ea7-4f1d-aedf-111b6c16e68c
provisioner: kubernetes.io/cinder
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: WaitForFirstConsumerтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН

Create a persistent volume claim from this template:

apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
 name: vol-teste-vai
spec:
 accessModes:
┬а - ReadWriteOnce
 resources:
┬а┬а requests:
┬а┬а┬а┬а storage: 10Gi
 storageClassName: standardтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН

As the volumeBindingMode is set to WaitForFirstConsumer, we had to actually attach the PV to a deployment in order to the Cinder volume to be created. For that, we used OpenShift GUI:

From OpenShift side:

cloud-user@bastion ~]$ oc get pv
NAME┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а CAPACITY┬а┬а ACCESSMODES┬а┬а RECLAIMPOLICY┬а┬а STATUS┬а┬а┬а CLAIM┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а STORAGECLASS┬а┬а REASON┬а┬а┬а AGE
pvc-691a2bbb-0d5a-4d2f-94e2-f50708085c7d┬а┬а 10Gi┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а RWO┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а Delete┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а Bound┬а┬а┬а┬а default/vol-teste-vai┬а┬а standard┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а 55sтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН

From OpenStack side, the actual Cinder volume created dynamically by OpenShift:

(overcloudrc) [cloud-user@bastion ~]$ openstack volume list
+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+-----------+------+-------------+
| ID┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а | Name┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а | Status┬а┬а┬а | Size | Attached to |
+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+-----------+------+-------------+
| b75c57b4-ea8f-4d73-b0d6-28ab02458842 | lab01-9v54g-dynamic-pvc-691a2bbb-0d5a-4d2f-94e2-f50708085c7d | available |┬а┬а 10 |┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а |
+--------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+-----------+------+-------------+тАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН

Swift Integration

Worked out of the box.

From OpenStack side:

(overcloud) [stack@manager ~]$ openstack container list
+----------------------------------------------------------------+
| Name┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а┬а |
+----------------------------------------------------------------+
| lab01-9v54g-image-registry-phbhqxrotxhvfkunvehpebfyeyajvoiirer |
+----------------------------------------------------------------+тАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАН

We can see the docker registry images inside the container:

(overcloud) [stack@manager ~]$ swift list lab01-9v54g-image-registry-phbhqxrotxhvfkunvehpebfyeyajvoiirer | grep registry
files/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/02/02c05272c8dd4f99d68e12066db506412bef3c460dbb4a587a7cafc8af593e35/data
files/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/45/455ea8ab06218495bbbcb14b750a0d644897b24f8c5dcf9e8698e27882583412/data
files/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/46/46fc24a071a44b29a3ba49c94f75a47514a56470d539c9204f3e7688973fc93a/data
files/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/4a/4abfcf58ff10724f3886be18423f80a481e90c4b258fbecad6ea06917a266003/data
files/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/56/566b3d0a5bcd5903e86174ab1551a402b1c0dade2aa918d86895516bd2d0dd43/data
files/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/6d/6d3329d5faa944944d54166b52dd7d11fcb99e6467950b5679095a44346c1cc8/data
files/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/81/8170b35922ece1799b004d6fe41e253808d47da894197d3c7fe2b49614336fa7/data
files/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/84/84e620d0abe585d05a7bed55144af0bc5efe083aed05eac1e88922034ddf1ed2/data
files/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/b5/b57725894ce17afd25fefcef7fdb2467837e85834bf482759b9ca67b261a3724/data
files/docker/registry/v2/blobs/sha256/bb/bb13d92caffa705f32b8a7f9f661e07ddede310c6ccfa78fb53a49539740e29b/data
files/docker/registry/v2/repositories/openshift/python/_layers/sha256/455ea8ab06218495bbbcb14b750a0d644897b24f8c5dcf9e8698e27882583412/link
files/docker/registry/v2/repositories/openshift/python/_layers/sha256/46fc24a071a44b29a3ba49c94f75a47514a56470d539c9204f3e7688973fc93a/link
files/docker/registry/v2/repositories/openshift/python/_layers/sha256/4abfcf58ff10724f3886be18423f80a481e90c4b258fbecad6ea06917a266003/link
files/docker/registry/v2/repositories/openshift/python/_layers/sha256/8170b35922ece1799b004d6fe41e253808d47da894197d3c7fe2b49614336fa7/link
files/docker/registry/v2/repositories/openshift/python/_layers/sha256/84e620d0abe585d05a7bed55144af0bc5efe083aed05eac1e88922034ddf1ed2/link
files/docker/registry/v2/repositories/openshift/python/_layers/sha256/bb13d92caffa705f32b8a7f9f661e07ddede310c6ccfa78fb53a49539740e29b/link
files/docker/registry/v2/repositories/openshift/python/_manifests/revisions/sha256/566b3d0a5bcd5903e86174ab1551a402b1c0dade2aa918d86895516bd2d0dd4/тАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНтАНlink

Keystone Integration

Tricky when using self-signed certificates from OpenStack side. Openshift Keystone Identity Provider only works with https keystone endpoints.

From the other side, there are several issues for deploying OpenShift on top endpoints with self-signed certificates:

  • [OSP] allow retrieval of ignition files from behind an encrypted endpoint which uses a self-signed certificate┬а/ target release 4.4.0
  • [IPI][OSP] Machine-api cannot create workers on osp envs installed with self-signed certs / target release 4.4.0
  • [OSP] allow retrieval of ignition files from behind an encrypted endpoint which uses a self-signed certificate / target release 4.3.z
  • [IPI][OSP] Machine-api cannot create workers on osp envs installed with self-signed certs / target release 4.3.z

TBD

Neutron/Octavia Integration (Kuryr)

TBD

References

  • Red Hat OpenShift 4.2 IPI on OpenStack 13: All-in-one setup
  • Automated Infrastructure in the On-Premise Datacenter тАУ OpenShift 4.2 on OpenStack 15 (Stein)
  • OpenShift 4.2 on Red Hat OpenStack Platform 13
entry.summary: I would like to share my experience installing and integrating OpenShift with various OpenStack services, namely Cinder, Swift and Keystone (Neutron and Octavia TBD).

entry.title: Open Networking Foundation
entry.content:
Open Networking Foundation

The Open Networking Foundation (ONF) is a non-profit operator led consortium driving transformation of network infrastructure and carrier business models.

We are an open, collaborative, community of communities. The ONF serves as the umbrella for a number of projects building solutions by leveraging network disaggregation, white box economics, open source software and software defined standards to revolutionize the carrier industry.

ONF Ambassador Program

The ONF Ambassador Program empowers anyone knowledgeable and passionate about ONF projects and initiatives to promote these in their part of the world. The program provides a simple framework and tools to help organize or attend events, recruit and mentor new contributors, and support a local developer community.If you feel that you want to represent and promote ONF, its mission, and projects in your region, and inspire, recruit, and support new contributors, please apply here to become an ONF Ambassador.

Profile
